steps: Go to https://my.freshbooks.com/#/developer and log in with your FreshBooks account
Click 'Create an App' in the top-right corner
Enter a unique Application Name (this appears on the FreshBooks app store and user integrations page)
Enter a Redirect URI — this must be an HTTPS URL (localhost is allowed during development)
Optionally fill in Description, Application Homepage, and other metadata
Your Client ID and Client Secret are now displayed on the application detail page
Copy both values — the Client Secret is shown here and can be retrieved later from the same page

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID for your FreshBooks application

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 client secret for your FreshBooks application. Keep this confidential.

notes: FreshBooks requires OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow — no simple API key is available
Access tokens expire after 12 hours; use the refresh token to get a new one
FreshBooks offers a 30-day free trial; active subscription required for API access beyond that
Application name must be globally unique across all FreshBooks apps
The developer portal is at my.freshbooks.com/#/developer (hash-routed SPA)

test_endpoint.description: Retrieve the authenticated user's FreshBooks profile and associated business memberships
